@@ -0,0 +1,71 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "keycloak-14122-refresh-token-rotation-with-multiple-tabs",
+  "missionClass": "fixer",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "keycloak: Refresh token rotation with multiple tabs",
+    "description": "Refresh token rotation with multiple tabs. This issue affects 78+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify keycloak troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your keycloak installation:\n```bash\nkc.sh --version 2>/dev/null || bin/kc.sh --version\n```\nLook for errors or warnings that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review keycloak configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant keycloak configuration:\n### Describe the bug\n\nFollowing this [discussion](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/discussions/10937), we are experience the problem."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Refresh token rotation with multiple tabs",
+        "description": "As far as I can see, nothing is being shared between the tabs apart from the HTTP only cookie on the login endpoint (which only keycloak has control of). Each tab is going to the auth provider login endpoint and getting different id_tokens and refresh_tokens, but for some reason they are still treated as the same session so when one tab request a new token, it invalidates the other trying to get a new one.\n\nHence why I think this is a bug that needs to be fixed in the keycloak API as it's not\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm Refresh token rotation with multiple tabs is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\nTest keycloak to confirm the issue is resolved.\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "As far as I can see, nothing is being shared between the tabs apart from the HTTP only cookie on the login endpoint (which only keycloak has control of).",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "keycloak",
+      "incubating",
+      "security",
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "keycloak"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "advanced",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "troubleshoot"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "incubating",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/14122",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak"
+    },
+    "reactions": 78,
+    "comments": 38,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "tools": [
+      "java"
+    ],

+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-04-01T06:42:40.706Z",

+}

@@ -0,0 +1,72 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "keycloak-14122-refresh-token-rotation-with-multiple-tabs",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "keycloak: Refresh token rotation with multiple tabs",
+    "description": "Refresh token rotation with multiple tabs. This issue affects 77+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify keycloak troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your keycloak deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n keycloak -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keycloak\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keycloak -n keycloak --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review keycloak configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant keycloak configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n keycloak -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keycloak\nkubectl get configmap -n keycloak -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=keycloak\n```\n### Describe the bug\n\nFollowing this [discussion](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/discussions/10937), we are experience the problem."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Refresh token rotation with multiple tabs",
+        "description": ">  If each tab does its own authentication request, wouldn't that then result in different client sessions? Or, do we end up with a single client session for multiple tabs? Reason I'm asking is that sharing a refresh token as suggested here through sessionStorage is effectively resulting in actual invalid re-use of a refresh token, while if a refresh token is not stored in sessionStorage and each that does an authentication/authorization request on their own they would then have different\n\nSee the fix PR for details: linked PR"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm Refresh token rotation with multiple tabs is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=keycloak -n keycloak --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n keycloak --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": ">  If each tab does its own authentication request, wouldn't that then result in different client sessions? Or, do we end up with a single client session for multiple tabs? Reason I'm asking is that sharing a refresh token as suggested here through sessionStorage is effectively resulting in actual invalid re-use of a refresh token, while if a refresh token is not stored in sessionStorage and each",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
